skill.name: residency-interview-prep
skill.description: Mock interview preparation tool for residency Match interviews. Generates realistic behavioral, clinical, program-specific, and ethical questions with STAR-format guidance.
skill.summary: AI-powered mock interview question generator for medical residency Match preparation with structured feedback.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 43 static analysis findings are false positives after human review. The skill is a legitimate residency interview preparation tool containing a self-contained Python script (no I/O, no network, no external dependencies) and a SKILL.md instruction file. The external_commands risk factor is present because the skill instructs the AI to run Python scripts via documented shell commands, but no malicious execution patterns were found. No prompt injection detected. Legacy audit scored the skill 85/100 Production Ready with all security gates passing.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged plain text strings as weak cryptographic algorithms. Lines identified include YAML frontmatter descriptions, markdown table headers, and dictionary values in scripts/main.py. None contain cryptographic operations.

high_findings.title: Path Traversal Sequence - False Positive
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged ../ patterns that appear inside a security checklist as examples of what to prevent. The text reads 'No unauthorized file system access (../)' and 'Input file paths validated (no ../ traversal)' - these are documenting safety requirements, not implementing traversal.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution in Documentation - Intentional Feature
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected 26 instances of Ruby/shell backtick execution in SKILL.md. These are markdown-fenced code blocks (```bash) containing example commands like 'python scripts/main.py'. This is expected documentation for a skill that includes a Python component. The external_commands risk factor is valid but represents intentional, documented functionality.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub URL - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged a GitHub URL on SKILL.md line 7. This is the legitimate source repository URL for the skill, displayed as a citation. No data exfiltration or unauthorized network access.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Descriptions - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged metadata text in audit_result_v1.json and workflow guidance text in SKILL.md as system reconnaissance. These are descriptions of the skills functionality, scope boundaries, and test case descriptions. No actual recon commands or system enumeration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Code Block with Shell Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md contains fenced code blocks with example bash commands. These instruct the AI to run 'python scripts/main.py' and 'python -m py_compile scripts/main.py'. While this is the intended documentation pattern for a skill with a Python component, the AI is expected to execute these commands.

content.user_title: Prepare for Residency Match Interviews with Mock Questions
content.value_statement: Residency interview preparation requires repeated practice with realistic questions and structured feedback. This skill generates mock interview questions across behavioral, clinical, program-specific, and ethical categories with STAR-format guidance and common pitfalls.
actual_capabilities: Generates behavioral interview questions in STAR format with key points and common pitfalls
Provides clinical scenario questions testing ethical decision-making and patient management
Creates program-specific questions tailored to residency programs
Offers ethical dilemma questions covering legal and professional boundaries
Produces multi-question practice sessions combining all question types
Includes specialty-specific considerations for tailored responses
limitations: Cannot provide real-time interview coaching or personalized feedback on spoken responses
Question bank is static and does not include program-specific details from actual residency programs
Does not simulate follow-up questions or multi-turn interview conversations
Cannot verify the accuracy of specialty-specific medical knowledge in user responses

use_cases.title: Self-Guided Interview Practice
use_cases.description: Generate practice questions and review suggested response structures. Use the key points and pitfalls to evaluate and improve your own answers.
use_cases.target_user: Medical students preparing for residency interviews independently

use_cases.title: Interview Workshop Facilitation
use_cases.description: Use the skill to generate question sets for group interview practice sessions. Discuss common pitfalls and ideal response structures with peers or mentors.
use_cases.target_user: Residency advisors and career counselors running interview workshops

use_cases.title: Structured Response Analysis
use_cases.description: Generate a behavioral or ethical scenario question and compare your planned response against the suggested STAR structure and key points to identify gaps.
use_cases.target_user: International medical graduates new to US residency interview formats

prompt_templates.title: Quick Behavioral Question
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a behavioral interview question for residency using the residency-interview-prep skill. Give me a question and the suggested STAR structure.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick practice session between other daily tasks

prompt_templates.title: Full Practice Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a full practice session with the residency-interview-prep skill. Generate 5 questions covering behavioral, clinical, program, and ethical categories. Show the question, suggested structure, key points, and common pitfalls for each.

prompt_templates.title: Specialty-Focused Practice
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the residency-interview-prep skill to generate program-specific and clinical questions for a surgery residency applicant. Include specialty considerations in the output.

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Response Evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the residency-interview-prep skill, generate an ethical dilemma question. After I provide my response, evaluate it against the suggested structure, key points, and common pitfalls. Identify strengths and areas for improvement.

output_examples.input: Give me one behavioral question for residency interviews.
output_examples.output:
Tell me about a time you made a medical error.

Category: behavioral
Suggested Structure: STAR: Situation, Task, Action, Result + reflection

Key Points:
- Demonstrate accountability
- Emphasize patient safety as the priority
- Show what you learned from the mistake
- Describe system improvements you contributed to

Common Pitfalls:
- Blaming others for the error
- Minimizing the seriousness of the mistake
- Not reflecting on what was learned

output_examples.input: Generate a clinical scenario question for an internal medicine interview.
output_examples.output:
A patient refuses your recommended treatment. How do you proceed?

Category: clinical
Suggested Structure: Assess capacity to Educate to Explore concerns to Shared decision-making

Key Points:
- Respect patient autonomy in decision-making
- Ensure the patient fully understands the risks and benefits
- Address cultural, religious, or personal barriers
- Document the discussion thoroughly

Common Pitfalls:
- Attempting to coerce the patient
- Dismissing the patients concerns
- Not offering alternative treatment options

output_examples.input: Run a practice session for ethical questions.
output_examples.output:
A 17-year-old wants an abortion but does not want parents to know. How do you handle this?

Category: ethical
Suggested Structure: Review legal requirements to Apply ethics principles to Patient-centered approach

Key Points:
- Know your state laws regarding minor consent and reporting
- Understand minor confidentiality rights and exceptions
- Provide counseling without personal bias
- Assess for patient safety concerns

Common Pitfalls:
- Letting personal bias affect the consultation
- Not knowing the legal requirements in your jurisdiction
- Breaking confidentiality in a way that is not legally permitted

best_practices: Start with behavioral questions to build confidence before moving to clinical and ethical scenarios that require more detailed medical knowledge.
Use the key points and pitfalls as a self-evaluation checklist after answering each question to identify specific areas for improvement.
Practice the same question multiple times, gradually reducing reliance on the suggested structure to build natural response fluency.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill as a substitute for reviewing actual program-specific information from residency websites or the NRMP.
Do not skip the common pitfalls section - understanding what to avoid is as important as knowing what to include.
Do not attempt to use this skill for diagnosing medical conditions or making clinical decisions during the interview.

faq.question: How many questions are available in the question bank?
faq.answer: The skill contains questions across four categories: behavioral, clinical, program-specific, and ethical.

faq.question: Does this skill provide feedback on my actual spoken responses?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides suggested response structures, key points, and common pitfalls. You must self-evaluate your own responses against these guidelines.

faq.question: Can this skill simulate a full interview with follow-up questions?
faq.answer: No. Each question is generated independently. The skill does not simulate multi-turn conversations or adaptive follow-up questions.

faq.question: Does this skill require internet access to run?
faq.answer: No. The Python script runs locally with a built-in question bank. No external API calls or network access is needed.

faq.question: Can I customize the questions for my specific specialty?
faq.answer: Yes. You can pass a specialty parameter to add specialty-specific considerations to the generated questions.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for practicing with a partner or group?
faq.answer: Yes. You can generate question sets and use them for group practice sessions, with each person taking turns answering.
